title: Dianthus lereschii
taxonomy: Dianthus lereschii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Dianthus, which includes other species of carnations and pink flowers. The plant's classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Dianthus lereschii is a rare and endangered species, which adds to the complexity of its classification. Its unique characteristics and limited distribution have contributed to its classification as a distinct species within the Dianthus genus.

intro: Dianthus lereschii, also known as Leresch's pink or pink gillyflower, is a flowering plant belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Dianthus lereschii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dianthus lereschii is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is commonly used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
